top_signals.title: Shipping rebounds in Strait of Hormuz one week after U.S.-Iran deal – but fragile confidence threatens recovery
top_signals.summary: Shipping traffic through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz is recovering one week after the U.S. and Iran signed an interim peace deal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Asian LNG Buyers Expect Qatar to Let Force Majeure Lapse in July
top_signals.summary: Asian liquefied natural gas customers of Qatar expect the supplier to let its force majeure expire in the middle of next month, as tensions in the region ease and the Persian Gulf producer ramps up exports.
